templates.text: For a heartbeat, the walls dissolve. You see {echoText} — impossibly distant, impossibly clear. Then stone again.
The dungeon shifts. This corridor was not here before. It remembers {echoText}.
{echoText}. You have never been there. But something in your marrow says otherwise.
Carvings on the wall. A map. {echoText} is marked. So is where you stand now. The distance between them is shrinking.
{echoText} exists somewhere above. Down here, a copy exists — older, heavier, built from what the place looked like before history cleaned it up.
{echoText} exists above. Down here, it exists differently. You will see what that means before you leave.
The walls know {echoText}. Not from memory — from age. Places this old absorb the names of other places like water absorbs salt.
{echoText} is recorded now — added to the dungeon's internal geography. It does not ask permission.
The stone recognizes {echoText}. It has heard the name before. It does not say from whom.
Some of this dust came from {echoText}. Carried here by means that require more explanation than the dungeon will provide.
The corridor was longer than it should be. {echoText} has that effect on distances — places that know it stretch themselves, as if trying to reach.
{echoText}. Added to the record. The dungeon is a better map than it was before you said that.
